channel.title: RNS News — Equity & Markets Insight
channel.description: Summarised UK regulatory news with priority levels

item.title: Whitbread PLC — Whitbread director Kal Atwal to join Quilter plc's board as an independent non-executive director from 1 September 2026.
item.description: [LOW] Kal Atwal to be appointed an independent non-executive director at Quilter plc , effective 1 September 2026 , also joining its Remuneration Committee. Routine external-directorship disclosure; no further disclosures required under UKLR 6.4.9R and no impact on Whitbread's trading or guidance.

item.title: Primary Health Properties PLC — PHP will report interim results for the six months to 30 June 2026 on Thursday 30 July 2026, with an analyst webcast at 11.30am BST.
item.description: [LOW] Primary Health Properties PLC will publish interim results for the six months ended 30 June 2026 on Thursday, 30 July 2026 . Virtual analyst and investor presentation at 11.30am BST on 30 July via live webcast and conference call, followed by a managed Q&A; recording available from c.3.00pm BST. Routine scheduling notice with no financial figures, trading detail or guidance — the numbers follow on

item.title: Paragon Banking Group PLC — Paragon Banking Group issues £200m of Green Tier 2 Bonds at a 6.5% coupon, upsized on 2.8x oversubscribed demand.
item.description: [MEDIUM] Paragon Banking Group PLC has completed issuance of £200 million of Green Tier 2 Bonds , upsized from an initial £175 million . Coupon of 6.5% , maturing September 2036 , priced 205 basis points above the UK five-year gilt — described as the tightest reset spread for any sub-benchmark sterling Tier 2 issue. Final orderbook was 2.8x oversubscribed ; the 205bp spread compares with 396bp on the Tier

item.title: JD Sports Fashion PLC — JD Sports appoints Darren Shapland as Interim Chair following the 2026 AGM, pending appointment of a permanent Chair.
item.description: [MEDIUM] Darren Shapland appointed Interim Chair with immediate effect after the 2026 AGM on 21 July 2026 , until a permanent Chair is found. Follows Andrew Higginson 's intention to step down as Chair, flagged in the announcement dated 22 April 2026 . Shapland steps down as Chair of the ESG Committee (remains a member) and temporarily leaves the Audit & Risk Committee , now chaired by Sarah Kuijlaars . Pr

item.title: Quilter plc — Quilter appoints Kal Atwal and Kan Kotecha as independent NEDs from 1 September 2026, while Moira Kilcoyne steps down on 31 December 2026.
item.description: [MEDIUM] Kal Atwal and Kan Kotecha appointed independent Non-executive Directors of Quilter plc , effective 1 September 2026 . Kal Atwal (ex-founding MD of comparethemarket.com, 16 years at BGL Group) joins the Remuneration Committee ; Kan Kotecha (CTO at CAIS, formerly 20+ years at Morgan Stanley and VP Engineering at Google) joins the Risk Committee . Moira Kilcoyne , on the Board since December 2016, wi

item.title: OSB Group PLC — OSB non-executive director Kal Atwal appointed as an independent non-executive director at Quilter plc from 1 September 2026.
item.description: [LOW] Kal Atwal , Independent Non-Executive Director at OSB GROUP PLC , has been appointed an Independent Non-Executive Director at Quilter plc with effect from 1 September 2026 . She will also join Quilter's Remuneration Committee on the same date. Routine external-directorship disclosure under UKLR 6.4.9R with no impact on OSB's trading or guidance.

item.title: Ocado Group PLC — Ocado wins agreement to build a large automated Customer Fulfilment Centre for a fast-growing European national retailer, going live in FY28.
item.description: [HIGH] Ocado Group plc has signed an agreement to build a large, automated Customer Fulfilment Centre (CFC) for an unnamed fast-growing European national retailer , due to go live in FY28 . Site to be fitted with Ocado's Re:Imagined technology suite, including the 600s bot , On-Grid Robotic Pick (OGRP) and a fully Automated Freezer . Retailer expects to move existing online volumes into the CFC quickly,

item.title: OSB Group PLC — OSB Group non-executive director Kal Atwal to join Quilter plc board as an independent non-executive director from 1 September 2026.
item.description: [LOW] Kal Atwal , Independent Non-Executive Director of OSB GROUP PLC , has been appointed an Independent Non-Executive Director at Quilter plc with effect from 1 September 2026 . She will also join Quilter's Remuneration Committee on the same date. Routine UKLR 6.4.9R external-directorship disclosure with no impact on OSB's trading or guidance; no further disclosures required.

item.title: Ocado Group PLC — Ocado signs a deal to build a large automated Customer Fulfilment Centre for a fast-growing European national retailer, going live in FY28.
item.description: [HIGH] Ocado Group PLC has signed an agreement to build a large automated Customer Fulfilment Centre (CFC) for an unnamed fast-growing European national retailer , due to go live in FY28 . Site will use Ocado's Re:Imagined technology suite, including the 600s bot , On-Grid Robotic Pick (OGRP) and a fully Automated Freezer . Retailer expects to transfer existing online order volumes quickly, with the CFC

item.title: JD Sports Fashion PLC — JD Sports Fashion passed all 23 resolutions on a poll at its 2026 AGM held in Manchester on 21 July 2026.
item.description: [LOW] All resolutions 1 to 23 at JD Sports Fashion Plc 's AGM were duly passed on a poll by the requisite majorities on 21 July 2026 . Routine AGM voting-results disclosure with no impact on trading or guidance; full poll results available on the Company's website at www.jdplc.com.

item.title: Supermarket Income Reit PLC — Supermarket Income REIT has raised £100m via a placing and retail offer to help fund a ~£216m acquisition of nine grocery assets.
item.description: [HIGH] Supermarket Income REIT (LSE: SUPR) has raised gross proceeds of £100m through a placing and retail offering of new shares. Net proceeds plus leverage will fund the acquisition of nine assets for approximately £216m , expected to be EPS accretive in the first full year . Management says the enlarged issue should aid share trading liquidity and broaden shareholder appeal in a market where scale mat

item.title: Paragon Banking Group PLC — Paragon Banking Group has issued £200m of Green Tier 2 Bonds at a 6.5% coupon, upsized on 2.8x oversubscribed demand.
item.description: [HIGH] Paragon Banking Group PLC has completed the issuance of £200 million of Green Tier 2 Bonds. Coupon of 6.5% , maturity September 2036 , priced 205 basis points above the UK five-year gilt — described as the tightest reset spread for any sub-benchmark sterling Tier 2 issue. Transaction upsized from an initial £175 million on strong demand; final orderbook 2.8x oversubscribed . The 205bps spread comp

item.title: Whitbread PLC — Whitbread discloses that director Kal Atwal will join Quilter plc's board as an independent non-executive director from 1 September 2026.
item.description: [LOW] Whitbread PLC discloses that Kal Atwal will be appointed an independent non-executive director at Quilter plc with effect from 1 September 2026 . Kal Atwal will also join Quilter's Remuneration Committee on the same date. Routine external-directorship disclosure under UKLR 6.4.9R , with no impact on Whitbread's trading or guidance; no further disclosures required.

item.title: Berkeley Group Holdings PLC — Berkeley Energia reports maiden inferred resource of 11.8Mt at 0.41% Li2O and 0.21% Rb2O at its 100%-owned Conchas lithium-rubidium project in Spain.
item.description: [MEDIUM] Berkeley Energia reports a maiden Mineral Resource Estimate for its 100%-owned Conchas Project in Salamanca, Spain , part of its Critical Minerals Exploration Initiative. Maiden inferred MRE of 11.8Mt at 0.41% Li2O and 0.21% Rb2O , for ~49,000t of contained Li2O and ~25,200t of contained Rb2O (above a US$100/t NSR cut-off). 100% of the resource sits in the Inferred category; prepared by independen

item.title: Capricorn Energy PLC — Capricorn Energy posts scheme document for its recommended US$4.74-per-share cash takeover by Genel's Bidco, with shareholder meetings set for 18 August 2026.
item.description: [HIGH] Capricorn Energy plc has published and posted the Scheme Document for its recommended cash acquisition by Genel Energy No.9 Limited (Bidco) , indirectly owned by Genel Energy plc . Terms: US$4.74 per Capricorn share in aggregate — comprising US$3.75 cash plus a US$0.99 special (Permitted) dividend expected to be declared before the Effective Date. Court Meeting and General Meeting to be held at As

item.title: IP Group PLC — IP Group rejects revised Railpen takeover proposal worth 71.3p per share as undervaluing the company, but grants a 7-day PUSU extension for a higher offer.
item.description: [HIGH] IP Group plc confirmed it received a Revised Proposal from Railpen on 15 July 2026 to acquire the entire issued share capital, which the Board has unanimously rejected as continuing to significantly undervalue the company. Terms comprised 61.0 pence cash per share, a pro rata share of IP Group's stake in Oxford Nanopore Technologies plc valued at 10.3 pence , plus a Contingent Value Right of up to

item.title: Compass Group PLC — Compass Group posts 7.1% Q3 organic revenue growth and reaffirms confidence in full-year underlying operating profit growth above 11% in constant currency.
item.description: [HIGH] Organic revenue growth of 7.1% in Q3 (quarter ended 30 June 2026 ), with net new business growth accelerating into the 4-5% target range. Continues to expect underlying operating profit growth above 11% in constant currency for the full year. Client retention at 96% ; $4.3bn of new business wins over the last 12 months, up 16% year-on-year , with 50% from first-time outsourcing. Business & Industr

item.title: Smiths Group PLC — Smiths completes a £760m pension buy-in with M&G, insuring all c.17,000 members of its largest defined benefit scheme with no extra contributions required.
item.description: [MEDIUM] Smiths Industries Pension Scheme completes a £760m buy-in with M&G , securing benefits for over 10,000 members and leaving all c. 17,000 members fully insured across five annuity policies. Transaction funded from the Scheme's existing assets — no additional contributions required from Smiths. Follows the £900m buyout of the TI Group Pension Scheme with Aviva , Legal & General , Pension Insurance C

item.title: Kier Group PLC — Kier expects FY26 revenue and profit at the top end of market expectations, with the order book up 8% to c.£11.9bn.
item.description: [HIGH] Kier Group plc now expects FY26 revenue and profit to be at the top end of market expectations , after strong second-half momentum. Order book grew 8% to c.£11.9bn (30 June 2025: £11.0bn ), securing over 90% of FY27 Group revenue (based on consensus FY27 revenue of £4.407bn ). Period-end net cash expected at c.£232m , up c. 14% on the prior year ( £204m ); average month-end cash of c.£11m versus £

item.title: Rosebank Industries plc — Rosebank upgrades full-year 2026 expectations, with Group adjusted operating profit and EPS now seen ahead of consensus after strong early performance from MW Components and CPM.
item.description: [HIGH] Full-year 2026 expectations upgraded : Group adjusted operating profit and EPS now expected ahead of Company-compiled analysts' consensus (2026 consensus adjusted operating profit $294m ; 2027 $452m ). MW Components (acquired 28 May 2026 ) trading ahead of pre-acquisition expectations and set to exceed current market expectations for 2026 ; MW head-office closure to cut central costs by at least $

item.title: GB Group plc — GBG says Q1 to 30 June 2026 was in line with Board expectations, with mid-single-digit revenue growth in core Identity and Location segments.
item.description: [HIGH] GB Group plc issued a Q1 trading update alongside its AGM : financial performance in the three months ended 30 June 2026 was in line with the Board's expectations . Revenue growth in the core Identity and Location segments was comfortably mid-single digit , driven by strong trading in EMEA Identity and momentum behind GBG Go , its AI-powered global identity platform. Smallest segment Global Fraud

item.title: Mitie Group plc — Mitie suspends its £100m share buyback with immediate effect following OCS's recommended cash offer for the company.
item.description: [HIGH] Mitie Group plc is suspending its £100m share buyback programme with immediate effect, following today's announcement by OCS Group International Limited of a recommended cash offer for Mitie. The buyback, launched on 14 October 2025 , had to date purchased 49m ordinary shares (2.5p each) at a cost of £81m . 3m of the acquired shares were used for the 2022 Save As You Earn scheme (vested February 2

item.title: Galliford Try Holdings PLC — Galliford Try appointed to all five regional lots of the new £9.5bn CPC Construction and Capital Works framework for up to eight years.
item.description: [HIGH] Galliford Try appointed to the new Crescent Purchasing Consortium (CPC) Construction and Capital Works framework, worth up to £9.5 billion . Named to all five regional lots across England, Wales and Scotland , with a maximum term of eight years . Covers new-build and refurbishment across education (schools, colleges, universities) and the wider public sector, including healthcare and civic infrast

item.title: Wickes Group PLC — Wickes reported 2.3% Q2 revenue growth with LFL gains in both Retail and Design & Installation, and remains comfortable with 2026 adjusted PBT consensus.
item.description: [HIGH] Q2 revenue up 2.3% , driven by continued volume growth, with Retail revenue up 1.8% year-on-year and Design & Installation delivering a fifth consecutive quarter of positive delivered-sales growth. Retail two-year LFL up 8.5% ; TradePro sales up 6% with active members up 9% to 671,000 ; DIY sales broadly flat and Click & Collect and Home Delivery up 7% . Design & Installation project numbers rose

item.title: Wizz Air Holdings PLC — Wizz Air's Group Managing Director Michael Delehant to leave on 31 July 2026, with COO and CCO reporting directly to the CEO.
item.description: [MEDIUM] Michael Delehant , Group Managing Director, will leave Wizz Air with effect from 31 July 2026 to pursue opportunities outside the organisation. Diarmuid O'Conghaile (Chief Operations Officer) and Ian Malin (Chief Commercial Officer) will now report directly to CEO József Váradi . Jingyuan Sun , expected to join as Chief Technical Officer , will also report directly to the CEO on joining. Managemen

item.title: London Stock Exchange Group PLC — LSEG plans to launch LSE 24, a new 24/5 trading venue for digital, algorithmic and agentic trading, with client testing by end-2026.
item.description: [MEDIUM] London Stock Exchange Group PLC plans to launch LSE 24 , a new 24/5 trading venue supporting digital, algorithmic and agentic trading, built on LSEG's market infrastructure. Venue available for client testing by end of 2026 , with Exchange Traded Products (ETPs) as the first asset class launching in H1 2027 , subject to regulatory approval; flexibility to expand into equities as the next step. LSE

item.title: Helical PLC — Helical returns about £12m to shareholders via a B Share Scheme at 9.72p per share, alongside a 105-for share consolidation.
item.description: [MEDIUM] Helical is returning approximately £12 million to ordinary shareholders through a B Share Scheme , paying 9.72 pence per ordinary share . 122,320,093 B Shares are being allotted and issued today , against 123,355,260 existing ordinary shares in issue at the record time (excluding 1,035,167 treasury shares). B Shares are to be redeemed tomorrow at 9.72 pence each, with proceeds dispatched or CREST

item.title: Caledonia Mining Corporation Plc — Caledonia's 2Q26 gold production of 17.4koz at Blanket Mine came in line with forecast, keeping it on track to meet 2H-weighted FY26 guidance.
item.description: [HIGH] Caledonia Mining Corporation Plc reported 2Q26 production at its 64%-owned Blanket Gold Mine in Zimbabwe of 17.4koz Au (100% basis), in-line with broker forecast ( 1Q26: 14.8koz; 2Q25: 21.1koz ). Plant feed grade rose to 2.9g/t (1Q26: 2.5g/t ), with grades averaging 3.1g/t in July to date; management expects grades to hold around this level for the rest of CY26 . Improvement reflects good progress

item.title: Mony Group PLC — MONY Group appoints Emma Lloyd as independent Non-Executive Director from 1 October 2026 as Sarah Warby retires after eight years.
item.description: [MEDIUM] Emma Lloyd appointed independent Non-Executive Director of MONY Group PLC with effect from 1 October 2026 , following a planned Board succession process. Lloyd will join the Audit, Remuneration, Nomination and Risk & Sustainability Committees from the same date, subject to regulatory references. Sarah Warby to retire and step down as Non-Executive Director from 2 October 2026 after eight years on

item.title: Johnson Matthey PLC — Johnson Matthey has completed the £1,325m sale of its Catalyst Technologies business to Honeywell and will return £1bn to shareholders.
item.description: [HIGH] Johnson Matthey Plc completed the sale of its Catalyst Technologies business to Honeywell on 17 July 2026 for an enterprise value of £1,325 million on a cash and debt-free basis. Board confirms intention to return £1 billion of net sale proceeds to shareholders (subject to shareholder approval), comprising an £800 million special dividend with share consolidation and a £200 million on-market share

item.title: Aston Martin Lagonda Global Holdings PLC — Aston Martin confirms it is in ongoing discussions with potential financing providers over its debt structure, responding to press coverage.
item.description: [HIGH] Aston Martin notes recent press coverage about its debt financing options and confirms the Board is in ongoing discussions with potential financing providers . Statement carries inside information status under UK MAR; Board says it "regularly considers the Company's capital structure and strategic options". Group reiterates focus on positive momentum in Q1 2026 and a material improvement in financ

item.title: Big Yellow Group PLC — Big Yellow reported Q1 revenue up 3%, with like-for-like occupancy rising 2.2ppts to 79.2% and net achieved rent up 2% to £36.45 per sq ft.
item.description: [HIGH] Big Yellow Group PLC posted Q1 revenue up 3% year on year, with like-for-like store revenue up 2% for the quarter ended 30 June 2026 . Like-for-like closing occupancy rose 2.2 ppts to 79.2% from 77.0% at 31 March 2026, though down 0.2 ppts on 30 June 2025; occupancy across all 113 stores grew by 161,000 sq ft (2.4% of MLA) versus a 47,000 sq ft gain a year earlier. Closing net achieved rent across

item.title: Segro PLC — Prologis makes a third, sweetened all-share proposal for SEGRO valuing it at ~£13.5bn plus a partial cash alternative, but the SEGRO board has rejected it.
item.description: [HIGH] Prologis, Inc. has made a Third Proposal to acquire SEGRO plc , offering 0.0890 new Prologis shares per SEGRO share (up 6.0% on its initial approach) plus a Partial Cash Alternative of up to £2.7bn (c.20% of consideration) at a fixed 1,000 pence per share , subject to pro-rata scale-back. The proposal values each SEGRO share at 993 pence (assuming a 20% cash election) and the whole company at appr

item.title: Frasers Group PLC — Frasers Group launches a new share buyback via Panmure Liberum to purchase up to 10 million ordinary shares for a maximum GBP80m.
item.description: [MEDIUM] Frasers Group plc has appointed Panmure Liberum Limited to run a share buyback programme on its behalf. Panmure Liberum may purchase up to 10,000,000 ordinary shares of 10p each, with aggregate purchase price capped at £80,000,000 . Programme runs from today until the trading day before the next AGM, currently expected mid-September 2026 ; operates under the authority granted at the 24 September 2

item.title: Steel Dynamics Inc — Steel Dynamics posted Q2 2026 net income of $534m ($3.69/share) on net sales of $6.1bn, up sharply from $299m a year earlier.
item.description: [HIGH] Steel Dynamics reported Q2 2026 net sales of $6.1bn and net income of $534m , or $3.69 per diluted share, up from $403m ($2.78) in Q1 and $299m ($2.01) a year earlier. Result was reduced by a $16m non-cash impairment tied to relocating the planned second satellite aluminium recycled slab centre from Arizona to Columbus, Mississippi . Steel operations operating income rose 30% sequentially to $721m

item.title: Caledonia Mining Corporation Plc — Caledonia's Blanket Mine produced 17,360 ounces of gold in Q2 2026, up 18% on Q1, and reaffirmed full-year guidance of 72,000-76,500 ounces.
item.description: [HIGH] Blanket Mine gold production of 17,360 ounces in Q2 2026 , up 18% on 14,767 ounces in Q1 2026, reflecting improving access to higher-grade areas. Full-year 2026 guidance reaffirmed at 72,000 to 76,500 ounces , with output weighted to the second half. Q2 2026 was lower than Q2 2025 , which benefitted from exceptional record grades; no comparative ounce figure for Q2 2025 is stated. Grades now track

item.title: DCC Energy plc — DCC plc has renamed itself DCC Energy plc, effective 17 July 2026, with shares trading under the new name from 20 July 2026.
item.description: [MEDIUM] DCC plc has changed its name to DCC Energy plc , registered at the Companies Registration Office on 17 July 2026 and now effective, following shareholder approval at the AGM on 16 July 2026 . Shares trade under the new name DCC Energy plc from 20 July 2026 ; the TIDM code 'DCC' , ISIN and Sedol are all unchanged . The rebrand reflects the group's positioning as a multi-energy sales and distributio

item.title: IP Group PLC — Railpen makes improved possible offer for IP Group worth 61p cash plus a pro-rata Oxford Nanopore stake and a contingent value right of up to 11.3p per share.
item.description: [HIGH] Railpen (Railways Pension Trustee Company Limited), holder of 18.4% of IP Group plc , has made two improved proposals to the Board since its 22 June 2026 approach — a Rule 2.4 possible offer, not yet a firm intention. Latest Improved Proposal: 61 pence cash per IP Group share, plus a pro rata share of IP Group's Oxford Nanopore Technologies (ONT) holding valued at 10.6 pence per share (based on ON

item.title: RHI Magnesita NV — RHI Magnesita will publish its 2026 half-year results, for the six months to 30 June 2026, on Friday 31 July 2026.
item.description: [LOW] RHI Magnesita will report 2026 half-year results on Friday 31 July 2026 , covering the six months to 30 June 2026 . Analyst presentation at 08:15 UK time (09:15 CET) on the day, via live webcast and conference call. Diary/notice item only — no financial figures, trading detail or guidance disclosed.

item.title: Aston Martin Lagonda Global Holdings PLC — Aston Martin confirms ongoing discussions with potential financing providers over its capital structure following press coverage of its debt options.
item.description: [HIGH] Aston Martin Lagonda responds to press coverage of its debt financing options , released as inside information . Board says it regularly considers the capital structure and strategic options , including ongoing discussions with potential financing providers . Focus remains on executing strategy and building on positive Q1 2026 momentum , with a material improvement in financial performance expecte

item.title: Standard Life PLC — Standard Life confirms publication of offering circulars for its £5bn EMTN programme and £350m Restricted Tier 1 contingent convertible notes.
item.description: [LOW] Standard Life plc has published two offering circulars, both dated 17 July 2026 , after the London Stock Exchange's International Securities Market confirmed no comments. Covers the £5,000,000,000 Euro Medium Term Note Programme and £350,000,000 Fixed Rate Reset Perpetual Restricted Tier 1 Contingent Convertible Notes . Application made for admission to trading on the LSE's International Securitie

item.title: Mitchells & Butlers PLC — Mitchells & Butlers has applied to admit 341,324 new ordinary shares issued under its Share Incentive Plan, with dealings expected from 22 July 2026.
item.description: [LOW] Mitchells & Butlers plc has applied to the London Stock Exchange to admit 341,324 ordinary shares of 8 13/24p each, allotted under its Share Incentive Plan (SIP) . New shares will rank equally with existing shares; admission expected at 8.00 am on 22 July 2026 — a routine employee-scheme disclosure with no impact on trading or guidance.

item.title: Johnson Matthey PLC — Johnson Matthey completes £1,325m sale of its Catalyst Technologies business to Honeywell, confirming a £1bn return to shareholders.
item.description: [HIGH] Johnson Matthey Plc has completed the sale of its Catalyst Technologies business to Honeywell Technologies for an enterprise value of £1,325 million on a cash and debt-free basis. Board confirms intention to return £1 billion of net proceeds to shareholders: £800 million special dividend with share consolidation and £200 million on-market buyback (subject to shareholder approval). Special dividend

item.title: Burberry Group PLC — Burberry publishes its FY 2027 first-quarter trading update, released via a linked PDF with a 9.00am investor presentation, but the scraped text carries no figures.
item.description: [HIGH] Burberry Group plc has released its FY 2027 first-quarter trading update , dated 17 July 2026 . The scraped page is a pointer notice only — the full statement sits in a linked PDF and the FCA National Storage Mechanism; no revenue, comparable-sales or profit figures are present in this text. Virtual investor and analyst presentation scheduled for 9.00am (UK time) today, with slides and an indexed

item.title: Greatland Resources Ltd — Greatland Resources will lodge its June Quarter 2026 Activities Report on 29 July 2026, with a shareholder webcast the same day.
item.description: [LOW] Greatland Resources Limited ( AIM:GGP , ASX:GGP ) will lodge its Quarterly Activities Report for the June Quarter 2026 with the ASX on Wednesday, 29 July 2026 . A webcast and Q&A for shareholders and analysts follows the same day at BST 01:30am , with a recording available afterwards. Routine scheduling notice with no impact on trading or guidance; the quarterly figures are not yet released.

item.title: International Consolidated Airlines Group, S.A. — IAG's 1.125% senior unsecured convertible bonds due 2028 will all be converted and satisfied in cash, leaving none outstanding.
item.description: [MEDIUM] International Consolidated Airlines Group confirmed that Conversion Rights have been exercised over all outstanding 1.125% senior unsecured convertible bonds due 2028 , following the 5 June 2026 Optional Redemption Notice under Condition 7(b)(ii). IAG has exercised its Cash Alternative Election , so the conversions will be settled by payment of the Cash Alternative Amounts rather than in shares —

item.title: Rentokil Initial PLC — Rentokil Initial Senior Independent Director John Pettigrew has been appointed a non-executive director of SSE plc from 1 December 2026.
item.description: [LOW] John Pettigrew , Senior Independent Director of Rentokil Initial plc , appointed to the board of SSE plc as a non-executive director with effect from 1 December 2026 . Routine disclosure under Listing Rule 6.4.9 with no impact on Rentokil's trading or guidance.

item.title: Bridgepoint Group PLC — Bridgepoint's H1 2026 underlying EBITDA jumped 78% to £227.3m, with fee-paying AUM up a third, reinforcing confidence in its raised €28bn fundraising target.
item.description: [HIGH] Bridgepoint Group plc reported record H1 2026 results, with underlying EBITDA up 77.6% to £227.3m (H1 2025: £128.0m ) at a 60.6% margin . Underlying management fee income +22.8% to £254.4m (incl. £21.7m catch-up fees); FRE +42.1% to £108.0m ; PRE +109.5% to £120.7m . Underlying diluted EPS 15.5p (H1 2025: 10.4p ); underlying PBT £197.5m (H1 2025: £103.7m ). On an IFRS basis PBT fell to £42.9m (H1

item.title: Ninety One PLC — Ninety One reports assets under management of £184.0bn at 30 June 2026, up from £171.8bn at end-March and £139.7bn a year earlier.
item.description: [HIGH] Ninety One plc confirms AUM of £184.0bn at 30 June 2026 . Up from £171.8bn at 31 March 2026 (end of prior financial year) and £139.7bn at 30 June 2025 . Announcement gives the headline AUM figure only — no detail on net flows, market movement or currency is provided. Routine quarterly AUM disclosure; no guidance or profit information included.

item.title: The Weir Group PLC — Weir director Barbara Jeremiah has retired from the board of Johnson Matthey Plc with effect from 16 July 2026.
item.description: [LOW] Barbara Jeremiah has retired from the Board of Johnson Matthey Plc , effective 16 July 2026 — a change in the external directorships of a Weir Group director. Routine notification of change in director's details with no impact on Weir's trading or guidance.
